sources: Avoma discovery call 22 Jun 2026 (Mårten: qualitative pain described, no quantified impact)
SF Next_Step__c 30 Jun (workshop planned, opportunity to build quantified case)
metrics.narrative: No quantified business case was captured in the discovery call. Mårten described operational pain (100% decentralized procurement across 35–40 ERP systems, 110–120 sites, no group-level visibility or compliance tooling, manual processes) but did not quantify cost, cycle time, savings opportunity, or ROI. He stated 'if the case is good enough, I think it's fine' regarding budget, implying an internal business case will be built but no numbers have been shared with the relevant stakeholder buyer acknowledged the pain ('we have nothing today, zero tools') but has not translated it into financial impact. The recommended move is to quantify the business case during the upcoming solution workshop by anchoring iContract, iSource, iRisk, and Merlin capabilities to measurable outcomes (contract cycle time reduction, sourcing event savings, supplier risk avoidance, Merlin negotiation agent ROI on tail spend). Without a quantified case, the ambitious Q3–Q4 quick-win target and the decentralized organizational structure make progression past Qualified uncertain.

sources: Avoma discovery call 22 Jun 2026 (Mårten engaged, but stated 'I am 4 weeks in role, limited influence')
SF OpportunityContactRole (Mårten Evaluator, Johan Decision Maker not yet engaged)
SF Next_Step__c 30 Jun (workshop planned with Johan, opportunity to build champion)
champion.narrative: No champion has been secured. Mårten Carlsson (Group Purchasing Excellence lead) is the primary contact and is engaged (led the discovery call, asked detailed questions, committed to internal mapping), but he explicitly stated he is 4 weeks into his role and has limited influence due to the newness of his position. He described himself as building the business case and prioritized activity list but acknowledged he lacks the political capital to mandate group-level adoption. He has not demonstrated advocacy (no introductions to EB, no internal selling, no pushing back on competitors) and has not yet opened access to Johan (his manager, the Decision Maker). Johan is expected at the upcoming workshop but has not engaged so far. Mårten's engagement is active (recent call, committed to next steps) but his champion strength is weak due to limited tenure, stated lack of influence, and no evidence of internal advocacy. The recommended move is to develop Mårten into a champion by equipping him with a strong business case and ROI narrative during the workshop, and to secure Johan as an executive sponsor by demonstrating feasibility and strategic fit during the same session.

sources: Avoma discovery call 22 Jun 2026 (Mårten: 'we have nothing today, zero tools', Scanmarket for eSourcing, internal development option mentioned)
Known insight: status quo, internal development
competition.narrative: The primary competitor is status quo: 100% decentralized procurement with local tools, no group-level solution, and Scanmarket for eSourcing in some sites. Mårten stated 'we have nothing today, zero tools' and described satisfaction with Scanmarket for eSourcing, reducing urgency for a group-level replacement. He also mentioned the option of internal development but acknowledged 'we don't have the expertise' to build procurement tools in-house. No external vendor competitors (Coupa, Ariba, Jaggaer, GEP) have been named. The competitive risk is buyer inertia: if the business case is weak or the workshop fails to demonstrate feasibility for Q3–Q4 quick-win deployment, the buyer may default to continuing with decentralized local tools rather than investing in group-level SaaS. The wedge is Merlin (AI negotiation agent) and iRisk (supplier risk visibility), neither of which exist in the current stack, plus the group-level visibility and compliance mandate that Johan and Mårten are tasked with delivering. The activation is to make the solution workshop a proof-of-feasibility moment: show a phased roadmap (iContract or iRisk quick win in Q4, full suite in 2027) that delivers measurable value without requiring immediate 35-ERP integration, and position Merlin as the differentiated AI capability that local tools and internal development cannot replicate.

sources: Avoma discovery call 22 Jun 2026 (Mårten: 'we have nothing today, zero tools', described decentralized complexity)
SF OpportunityContactRole (Mårten and Johan are pain owners per their titles and mandate)
identify_pain.narrative: The buyer articulated clear operational pain during the discovery call. Mårten stated 'we have nothing today, zero tools' for group-level procurement and described 100% decentralized procurement across 35–40 ERP systems and 110–120 sites with no central visibility, compliance tooling, or contract management. He explained that local buyers use their own tools (Scanmarket for eSourcing in some sites) but there is no group-level standardization, no risk management, and no AI/automation. He described the pain owner as himself (Group Purchasing Excellence lead) and his manager Johan (VP Group Excellence & Sustainability), whose mandate is to drive purchasing maturity and compliance across the group. The pain is organizational and operational (lack of visibility, compliance risk, manual processes, no leverage on group spend) but not yet quantified. Mårten acknowledged that the business case must be strong enough to justify group-level investment and overcome decentralized resistance, but he has not translated the pain into financial impact (cost, cycle time, savings opportunity). The pain is confirmed and buyer-owned; quantifying it is the gap.

sources: No evidence in Avoma calls, SF fields, or tasks
paper_process.narrative: No paper process, contracting mechanics, or procurement channel has been discussed. MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec, and ERP/systems integration scope have not been mentioned. No SI or partner involvement has been identified. The discovery call focused entirely on operational scope, pain, and solution fit; commercial and contracting logistics were not covered. Given the 35–40 ERP decentralized environment and 110–120 sites, integration complexity and InfoSec review will be material, but the buyer has not yet addressed this. The recommended move is to introduce the contracting and integration discussion during or immediately after the solution workshop, once the business case and module scope are locked, by asking Mårten and Johan to walk through their procurement process, legal/InfoSec gatekeepers, and ERP integration requirements.

sources: Avoma discovery call 22 Jun 2026 (Mårten: 'if the case is good enough, I think it's fine' but no EB named)
SF OpportunityContactRole Johan Wijk tagged Decision Maker, not yet engaged on calls
SF Next_Step__c 30 Jun (workshop will include Johan, opportunity to map EB)
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified. Mårten Carlsson (Group Purchasing Excellence lead) is the primary contact and stated 'if the case is good enough, I think it's fine' regarding budget, but he is 4 weeks into his role and described himself as having limited influence due to the newness of his position. He acknowledged that budget approval will require a strong business case but did not name who controls the commercial decision or signs off on group-level procurement investments. Johan Wijk (Vice President, Group Excellence & Sustainability) is tagged as Decision Maker in Salesforce Contact Roles and is Mårten's manager, but he has not appeared on any calls yet, and his role in budget authority is unconfirmed. No CFO, CPO, or executive sponsor has been mentioned. The discovery call focused on operational scope and pain; commercial and pricing conversations have not started. The recommended move is to map budget authority explicitly during the solution workshop by asking Mårten and Johan (if present) to walk through the approval chain for a group-level SaaS investment of this scope.

sources: Avoma discovery call 22 Jun 2026 (Mårten described internal mapping and business case dependency)
SF OpportunityContactRole (Johan Wijk Decision Maker, not yet engaged)
SF Next_Step__c 30 Jun (workshop will include Johan, opportunity to map process)
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is partially visible. The current motion is discovery into solution workshop into business case development. Mårten stated that he is building an internal prioritized long list of Purchasing Excellence activities and will map Zycus capabilities against that list. He mentioned 'if the case is good enough, I think it's fine' for budget, implying an internal approval gate tied to business case strength, but the approval chain beyond Mårten and Johan is unmapped. The discovery call (22 Jun) included only Mårten (buyer) and Casper (Zycus rep). Johan Wijk (VP, tagged Decision Maker) has not yet joined but is expected at the solution workshop per Next_Step__c. No procurement, legal, InfoSec, or executive sponsors have appeared. The buyer mentioned a 2–3 week window 'before holidays' to complete internal mapping, suggesting summer holidays may delay workshop scheduling into Q3, which would compress the timeline for the ambitious Q3–Q4 quick-win target. The process so far is informal and exploration-phase; no RFP, RFI, or structured vendor selection process has been mentioned. The recommended move is to confirm the approval chain and timeline gates during the workshop.

sources: Avoma discovery call 22 Jun 2026 (Mårten: 'explain how modules support Purchasing Excellence activities, what data is required, what dependencies exist')
SF Next_Step__c 30 Jun (workshop planned to address these criteria)
decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer articulated high-level evaluation priorities during the discovery call: (1) module capabilities and how they support Purchasing Excellence activities, (2) data requirements and dependencies across iContract, iSource, iRisk, iSupplier, and Merlin, (3) feasibility of quick-win deployment in Q3–Q4 2026 despite 35–40 ERP decentralized complexity, and (4) ease of local adoption across 110–120 sites with minimal central mandate. Mårten explicitly asked Zycus to 'explain how the modules support Purchasing Excellence activities' and show 'what data is required, what dependencies exist'. He also mentioned that the buyer is mapping their own Purchasing Excellence activities into a prioritized long list to inform the evaluation. However, formal decision criteria (weighted scorecard, RFP/RFI requirements, or structured test cases) have not been shared. The criteria are inferred from the discovery conversation and are buyer-initiated asks, not yet formalized. The solution workshop is the venue to confirm and deepen these criteria by walking through use cases, dependencies, and a feasibility roadmap for the quick-win timeline.

items.action: Schedule and execute the 60–90 minute solution workshop with Mårten, Johan (VP, DM), and Nishita (Zycus solution consultant) by 15 Jul before summer holidays. Agenda: (1) walk through iContract, iSource, iRisk, Merlin dependencies and data requirements per Mårten's ask, (2) demonstrate phased roadmap with Q4 pilot (iContract or iRisk quick win on 1–2 sites) to prove feasibility against 35-ERP complexity, (3) quantify business case (contract cycle time reduction, sourcing savings, supplier risk avoidance, Merlin negotiation ROI on tail spend) to justify group-level investment, (4) map budget approval chain and confirm Johan's role in commercial decision, (5) position Merlin as differentiated AI capability that local tools cannot replicate. Outcome: secure Johan as executive sponsor, equip Mårten with business case narrative, validate Q4 pilot scope.
items.trigger: Discovery call 22 Jun: Mårten asked for module dependencies, data requirements, and Purchasing Excellence activity mapping. Next_Step__c 30 Jun confirms workshop as immediate next milestone. Buyer mapping due 'within 2–3 weeks before holidays' creates urgency.
items.expected_effect: Advances deal from Qualified to Shortlisted by validating feasibility, quantifying business case, securing DM (Johan) engagement, and developing Mårten as champion. Mitigates timeline risk (summer holidays) and complexity risk (35-ERP environment) by proposing phased Q4 pilot.

items.action: During the solution workshop, explicitly map the budget approval chain by asking Mårten and Johan to walk through the commercial sign-off process for a group-level SaaS procurement investment of this scope. Identify the Economic Buyer (CFO, CPO, or executive sponsor) and confirm whether Johan has budget authority or is an influencer. If EB is external to the workshop attendees, request an introduction or a follow-on commercial discussion with the EB within 2 weeks post-workshop.
items.trigger: Discovery call 22 Jun: Mårten stated 'if the case is good enough, I think it's fine' regarding budget but did not name who controls commercial approval. No EB identified in Contact Roles or calls.
items.expected_effect: Maps Economic Buyer, validates budget authority, and de-risks commercial progression. Enables accurate forecast and pricing conversation timing.

items.action: Equip Mårten with a champion toolkit during the workshop: a one-page business case narrative (quantified ROI, phased roadmap, Merlin differentiation) and a stakeholder engagement plan (how to socialize the solution across decentralized sites, how to position group-level compliance mandate as the driver). Coach him to present the business case internally to Johan and other executives. Follow up within 1 week post-workshop to hear how internal conversations went and adjust the narrative as needed.
items.trigger: Discovery call 22 Jun: Mårten stated 'I am 4 weeks in role and have limited influence'. No evidence of champion behavior (advocacy, introductions, internal selling). Organizational buy-in risk due to decentralized structure.
items.expected_effect: Develops Mårten from weak to developing champion by giving him tools to sell internally. Increases probability of organizational buy-in and reduces political risk.

items.action: Position Merlin (AI negotiation agent) as the differentiated capability during the workshop by demonstrating a use case on tail-spend supplier negotiations (e.g., Merlin analyzes supplier price history, suggests negotiation tactics, automates counter-offers). Anchor it to measurable ROI (e.g., 5–10% savings on tail spend, 50% reduction in negotiation cycle time). Contrast with local tools (Scanmarket does eSourcing but no AI negotiation) and internal development (would take years and lack AI expertise). Make Merlin the Phase 2 centerpiece after Q4 pilot.
items.trigger: SF Products__c includes Merlin and Merlin Intake but discovery call did not surface buyer-led AI appetite. AIS fields unpopulated. Under-positioned on AI differentiation.
items.expected_effect: Elevates AI positioning from Curious to Hungry by demonstrating tangible Merlin ROI. Differentiates Zycus from status quo (local tools, Scanmarket) and internal development. Justifies group-level investment vs continuing with decentralized tools.

items.action: Propose a phased deployment roadmap during the workshop to address Q3–Q4 quick-win timeline vs 35-ERP complexity: Phase 1 (Q4 2026) = pilot iContract or iRisk on 1–2 high-priority sites with measurable KPIs (contract cycle time, supplier risk incidents); Phase 2 (Q1 2027) = expand to 5–10 sites and add Merlin negotiation agent; Phase 3 (2027) = group-level rollout across 110–120 sites with ERP integration. Show how Phase 1 delivers quick-win value without requiring full 35-ERP integration upfront, making adoption feasible and reducing organizational resistance.
items.trigger: Discovery call 22 Jun: Mårten stated 'we want to see quick wins in Q3 or Q4 this year' against 35–40 ERP decentralized complexity. Timeline is ambitious and creates execution risk.
items.expected_effect: Mitigates timeline and complexity risk by proposing realistic phased approach. Demonstrates feasibility and reduces buyer concern about ambitious Q3–Q4 target. Increases confidence in Zycus as executable partner vs status quo or internal development.

For a Jun 2027 close, required pace is approximately 30 days per major milestone across 12-month cycle. Currently on pace: discovery completed, workshop pending (the correct next milestone for Qualified stage). No forward slip detected (deal is 2 days old). Stage velocity appropriate for enterprise motion.

north_star_verdict.headline: Early-stage enterprise deal progressing appropriately. Discovery completed 22 Jun, next-step workshop scheduled for 60–90 min with DM (Johan) and primary contact (Mårten). Close date Jun 2027 allows full 12-month enterprise cycle. No forcing conditions active. Recent two-way engagement (9 days ago). Downgrade risks: no EB mapped, no champion secured, no quantified business case, ambitious Q3–Q4 quick-win expectation against 35–40 ERP complexity, and Mårten is 4 weeks in role with limited influence.

flags: Multi-thread beyond single contact: only Mårten Carlsson (Evaluator) engaged; Johan Wijk (Decision Maker, VP Excellence) invited to next workshop but not yet active; economic buyer unmapped; 2 buyer-side contact roles below multi-thread bar for enterprise deal

Quantify business case: pain clearly articulated (data transparency gap, time wasted, inability to consolidate spend) but no ROI, cycle-time savings, or cost-impact figures; buyer needs quantified case to secure organizational buy-in per 2026-06-29 call

Competitive insulation: no named vendor competitors but status quo (Scanmarket + decentralized local tools) and internal development are real alternatives; emphasize modular deployment, AI differentiation, and quick-win ROI to beat inertia

Economic Buyer not identified: Mårten stated budget approval depends on business case strength but did not name who controls commercial decision. Johan (VP, DM) has not engaged. Map EB during workshop.
Champion weak: Mårten engaged but 4 weeks in role with stated limited influence. No evidence of internal advocacy. Develop Mårten and secure Johan as executive sponsor during workshop.

No quantified business case: Operational pain confirmed but not translated to financial impact. Quantify savings, cycle time reduction, and ROI during workshop to justify group-level investment and Q3–Q4 quick-win timeline.

Ambitious timeline vs complexity: Q3–Q4 quick-win target against 35–40 ERP decentralized environment is aggressive. Recommend phased roadmap with Q4 pilot (iContract or iRisk) to demonstrate feasibility.

Key gaps: no EB mapped, no champion secured, no quantified business case, ambitious Q3–Q4 quick-win timeline against 35–40 ERP complexity. Mårten is 4 weeks in role with limited influence, creating champion risk. Workshop with Johan (DM) is the near-term gate to address gaps.

Buyer acknowledged internal development option but lacks expertise. Competitive risk is inertia: if business case is weak or quick-win feasibility is not demonstrated, buyer may default to status quo. Wedge is Merlin AI + iRisk + group visibility that local tools cannot deliver. Activation is phased roadmap with Q4 quick win.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Buyer expectations are aligned on operational scope (iContract, iSource, iRisk, Merlin for group-level visibility and compliance) but exposed on timeline and feasibility. Mårten expects Q3–Q4 2026 quick-win deployment against 35–40 ERP decentralized complexity, which is ambitious. He also expects clear module dependencies and data requirements, which Zycus has committed to show in the solution workshop. Fit is strong on capability (Merlin AI differentiation, iRisk supplier visibility) but at risk on execution speed and organizational adoption (100% decentralized, Mårten has limited influence, no EB/champion secured).

packets.subject: Johan Wijk

value.name: Status quo (decentralized procurement, local tools, Scanmarket for eSourcing)
value.quote: We have nothing today, zero tools. Some sites use Scanmarket for eSourcing and are satisfied with it.
value.how_we_win: Position Merlin (AI negotiation agent on tail spend) and iRisk (supplier risk visibility across 110–120 sites) as capabilities that decentralized local tools cannot replicate. Show group-level compliance and visibility mandate (Johan's and Mårten's charter) requires a centralized SaaS platform. Demonstrate phased roadmap with Q4 quick win (iContract or iRisk pilot) that delivers measurable value without requiring immediate 35-ERP full integration, making adoption feasible vs continuing with fragmented status quo.

packets.subject: Status quo (decentralized procurement, local tools, Scanmarket for eSourcing)

value.name: Internal development
value.quote: We don't have the expertise to build procurement tools in-house.
value.how_we_win: Buyer self-disqualified internal development due to lack of expertise. Reinforce this during workshop by showing depth of Zycus platform (iContract, iSource, iRisk, Merlin AI, iSupplier, Merlin Intake) and speed to value vs multi-year internal build with uncertain ROI.

value.name: Internal development
value.quote: Is it something we should develop ourselves? ... we are not experts within that specific topic
value.how_we_win: Emphasize speed-to-value (Q3–Q4 quick-win vs multi-year internal build), domain expertise (tested AI agents, procurement best practices, integration platform for 35–40 ERPs), and lower total cost of ownership (no ongoing maintenance burden).

packets.subject: Internal development
